body:
This PR delivers a comprehensive fix for anchor handling bugs in Python regex translation and adds a robust differential testing framework. The implementation is well-documented with clear explanations of the anchor semantics and case-splitting logic. The test corpus is thorough with 347 cases covering edge cases systematically. The typo fix, complement fix (intersecting with re.allchar()), and all anchor-splitting logic are sound. CI passes on all checks.

One improvement opportunity: The false,true and false,false concat cases have nearly identical anchor-splitting logic that could be extracted into a helper function to reduce duplication.

comments.body:
The `false, true` case (lines 247-255) and `false, false` case (lines 264-272) have nearly identical anchor-splitting logic. Both check `atStart && r2.containsAnchorStart && r1.hasNonAnchorContent` and produce the same union structure:

```lean
let r1b_eps := mkApp () (.op () reInterFunc.name none) [r1b, Core.emptyRegex]
let r2b_start := toCore r2 atStart atEnd
let r2b_mid := toCore r2 false atEnd
mkApp () (.op () reUnionFunc.name none)
  [mkApp () (.op () reConcatFunc.name none) [r1b_eps, r2b_start],
   mkApp () (.op () reConcatFunc.name none) [r1b, r2b_mid]]
```

Consider extracting this into a helper function that takes the pre-computed `r1b` and returns the union expression. This would reduce duplication and make the anchor-splitting pattern more explicit.
